llm_text:
{
  "recommendations": [
    {
      "rule_id": "rsu-double-count-001",
      "finding_id": "finding-rsu-double-count-001-RSU-VEST-001_RSU-VEST-002_RSU-VEST-003",
      "one_line_summary": "3 RSU vest lots show near-$0 basis — W-2 income likely double-counted as capital gain",
      "detail": "RSU vest income already hit Box 1 wages; the 1099-B cost basis must equal that W-2 inclusion, not zero. As-filed, the same income is taxed twice — once as wages and again as capital gain. Prior notes flagged RSU basis as a pain point. Correct basis on Form 8949 before filing.",
      "confidence": 0.93,
      "llm_only": false
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "depreciation-land-split-001",
      "finding_id": "finding-depreciation-land-split-001-rental-joliet-001",
      "one_line_summary": "Joliet rental depreciates full purchase price — land value never excluded, overstating deduction ~$2,818/yr",
      "detail": "Only the building is depreciable; land is never eligible under IRC §168. No land/building split exists in the depreciation schedule. Pull Will County assessor data to establish the correct split. At ~25% land, the annual over-deduction is roughly $2,818, creating both a current-year error and a future recapture exposure.",
      "confidence": 0.91,
      "llm_only": false
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "wash-sale-code-w-001",
      "finding_id": "finding-wash-sale-code-w-001-WASH-001_WASH-002_WASH-003",
      "one_line_summary": "3 broker lots missing Form 8949 Code W — $4,180 disallowed loss not added back, CP2000 risk",
      "detail": "The broker reported wash sale disallowances on these lots but Form 8949 column (f) lacks Code W and column (g) omits the addback. The IRS computer-matches broker 1099-B data and will issue a CP2000 when the disallowance isn't reflected. Fix columns f and g and add disallowed amount to replacement-lot basis.",
      "confidence": 0.95,
      "llm_only": false
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "salt-cap-tcja-2025-001",
      "finding_id": "finding-salt-cap-tcja-2025-001-global",
      "one_line_summary": "TY2025 SALT cap is $40K MFJ — return may be using old $10K cap, leaving ~$2,979 on the table",
      "detail": "The TCJA permanent extension raises the SALT cap to $40,000 MFJ starting TY2025. State and property taxes paid total $22,412, all deductible under the new cap vs. only $10,000 under prior law. Verify Schedule A line 5e reflects the $40K ceiling before filing.",
      "confidence": 0.88,
      "llm_only": false
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "hsa-8889-reconcile-001",
      "finding_id": "finding-hsa-8889-reconcile-001-person-olivia-mitchell",
      "one_line_summary": "Form 8889 line 6 shows $8,300 but TY2025 family HSA limit is $8,550 — deduction understated",
      "detail": "The statutory family HSA limit for TY2025 is $8,550. Line 6 is $8,300, causing lines 2 and 13 to also be understated. Correcting to $8,550 recovers up to $250 in additional deduction. Confirm actual contributions match and update all three linked lines.",
      "confidence": 0.9,
      "llm_only": false
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "passive-469-agi-phaseout-001",
      "finding_id": "finding-passive-469-agi-phaseout-001-global",
      "one_line_summary": "Rental loss $6,503 fully suspended — AGI $325,850 blows past $150K phaseout ceiling",
      "detail": "The §469(i) $25K rental allowance phases out completely above $150K AGI. At $325,850, the entire $6,503 rental loss is suspended and carried to Form 8582. No current-year deduction is available unless real-estate-professional status applies (see separate finding). Ensure the carryforward is correctly tracked.",
      "confidence": 0.97,
      "llm_only": false
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "estimated-tax-safe-harbor-110-001",
      "finding_id": "finding-estimated-tax-safe-harbor-110-001-global",
      "one_line_summary": "Withholding $6,352 short of 110% prior-year safe harbor — add Q4 estimated payment to avoid §6654 penalty",
      "detail": "Prior year AGI > $150K triggers the 110% safe harbor: $62,502 required, only $56,150 withheld. A Q4 estimated payment of $6,352 (or equivalent wage withholding increase) closes the gap. Failure to act means a small but avoidable underpayment penalty at filing.",
      "confidence": 0.9,
      "llm_only": false
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "passive-k1-nonpassive-002",
      "finding_id": "finding-passive-k1-nonpassive-002-[EIN_4fde52e3]",
      "one_line_summary": "Blackwood Trading Partners LP passive K-1 loss must be suspended on Form 8582, not deducted currently",
      "detail": "The K-1 is marked passive with a loss. With no offsetting passive income, the loss cannot be deducted and must be suspended on Form 8582 per IRC §469. If it was deducted on the current return, it needs to be removed and carried forward.",
      "confidence": 0.92,
      "llm_only": false
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "retirement-401k-headroom-003",
      "finding_id": "finding-retirement-401k-headroom-003-person-ryan-mitchell",
      "one_line_summary": "[PERSON_NAME_8d07d26d] has $17,500 of 401(k) headroom — maxing out saves ~$4,200 in taxes",
      "detail": "[PERSON_NAME_8d07d26d] has contributed only $6,000 of the $23,500 TY2025 §402(g) limit. Deferring the remaining $17,500 before year-end reduces taxable wages dollar-for-dollar. At the household's marginal rate this is roughly $4,200 in federal tax savings, plus state.",
      "confidence": 0.85,
      "llm_only": false
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "salt-cap-ptet-interaction-002",
      "finding_id": "finding-salt-cap-ptet-interaction-002-global",
      "one_line_summary": "PTET election on K-1 entities converts capped SALT cost into uncapped federal business deduction",
      "detail": "IRS Notice 2020-75 allows pass-through entities to pay state tax at the entity level, reducing K-1 income federally — effectively bypassing the SALT cap. Illinois and California elections are flagged separately. Coordinate with each partnership before their election deadline; benefit stacks per state.",
      "confidence": 0.78,
      "llm_only": false
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "passive-rep-consider-004",
      "finding_id": "finding-passive-rep-consider-004-rental-joliet-001",
      "one_line_summary": "Real-estate-professional status could unlock the fully-suspended $6,503 rental loss",
      "detail": "If either spouse logs 750+ hours in real property trades/businesses and materially participates in the Joliet rental, losses become nonpassive and bypass §469 limits entirely. Requires contemporaneous time logs. High-income households with one spouse in real estate commonly qualify. Worth a conversation before filing.",
      "confidence": 0.65,
      "llm_only": false
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "retirement-401k-headroom-003",
      "finding_id": "finding-retirement-401k-headroom-003-person-olivia-mitchell",
      "one_line_summary": "[PERSON_NAME_3f6faba4] has $9,500 of 401(k) headroom — deferring saves ~$2,280 in taxes",
      "detail": "[PERSON_NAME_3f6faba4] contributed $14,000 of the $23,500 TY2025 limit. Additional deferrals of up to $9,500 before year-end reduce Box 1 wages and federal tax by roughly $2,280 at the household's marginal rate. Coordinate with employer payroll to increase withholding rate for remaining pay periods.",
      "confidence": 0.85,
      "llm_only": false
    }
  ]
}
